name: Nango OAuth & Integration Platform API
what_it_does: Open-source OAuth and integrations platform with REST API — handling OAuth flows for 300+ APIs, token storage and refresh, and providing a unified integration layer with syncs and actions for connecting to external APIs without managing OAuth complexity.
use_cases: Managing OAuth connections to 300+ APIs (GitHub, Salesforce, Slack) without OAuth boilerplate
Building customer-facing integrations with automatic token refresh and storage
Syncing data from external APIs into your database via Nango sync scripts
Triggering actions on external APIs (create Jira ticket, send Slack message) via Nango actions
Self-hosted OAuth token management for privacy-conscious applications
not_for: Simple single-API OAuth (Nango shines with multiple APIs)
Teams needing fully managed integration platform without self-hosting option
Data transformation and ETL at Fivetran/Airbyte scale
best_when: A team needs to manage OAuth connections to multiple external APIs without building OAuth infrastructure, with option for self-hosting and open-source transparency.
avoid_when: You only integrate with one or two APIs where setting up Nango adds overhead vs. direct OAuth.

auth.notes: Nango secret key from dashboard. Used in SDK and REST API calls. Connection IDs for per-customer OAuth tokens. Self-hosted instances manage their own keys. Public key for frontend SDK.

pricing.paid_tiers: Starter from $250/month; Scale from $500; Enterprise custom; self-hosted: free
pricing.notes: MIT/ELv2 open source — free to self-host. Cloud pricing based on integrations and sync volume. Growing rapidly with strong developer adoption. YC W23. Good alternative to building OAuth infrastructure.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per plan; sync jobs rate-limited by underlying APIs

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Excellent documentation covering OAuth flows, syncs, and actions. TypeScript SDK for type-safe integration. Webhook support for sync completion and connection events. Open source means code is auditable.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Sync operations are idempotent — Nango tracks sync state and cursor. Action triggers create new operations. Connection management operations are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Custom sync scripts require TypeScript deployment to Nango — not pure REST configuration
Token refresh managed by Nango — but connection expiry still possible for some providers
Self-hosted requires Redis and PostgreSQL — non-trivial infrastructure for self-hosting
Per-integration rate limits inherited from underlying APIs — Nango doesn't bypass them

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. API key auth. SOC2 certified. GDPR compliant. Open source — fully auditable. OAuth tokens encrypted at rest. Self-hosted option for full data sovereignty.
